since_last: 37% of 101 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +1.99%; AI bubble risk 50 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: broad selling, 37% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX -0.02% · BTC -0.77% · Gold +1.90% · Nasdaq100 -0.30%. 37% of 101 AI names are green — broad selling. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +1.99%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Software & Cloud Infrastructure lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +1.99%; weakest is AI Software & Cloud Infrastructure at -3.78%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: SMCI is the standout at +21.02%
cards.body: Leaders: SMCI +21.02%, DELL +8.87%, CEG +4.16%, HPE +4.11%, NRG +3.71%. Laggards: PATH -13.46%, GEV -8.30%, NOW -7.04%, PLTR -6.72%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -4.02% at $118.57
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -4.02% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 50 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 50/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
